examples.title: Base Class with All Access Levels
examples.description: Demonstrates public, protected, and private members in a base class
examples.explanation: Base class demonstrates all three access specifiers. publicVar accessible everywhere, protectedVar accessible to derived classes, privateVar only within Base class.

examples.title: Public Inheritance Example
examples.description: Shows member accessibility with public inheritance
examples.explanation: In public inheritance: public stays public (accessible outside), protected stays protected (accessible to derived but not outside), private stays private (inaccessible).

examples.title: Private Inheritance Example
examples.description: Shows member accessibility with private inheritance
examples.explanation: In private inheritance: all members become private in derived class. Even public members cannot be accessed outside.

examples.title: Protected Inheritance Example
examples.description: Shows member accessibility with protected inheritance
examples.explanation: In protected inheritance: public and protected members become protected. Further derived classes can still access them.

examples.title: Constructor Execution Order
examples.description: Demonstrates proper constructor and destructor call sequence
examples.explanation: Base constructor runs first, then derived. Destructors run in reverse order (LIFO). This ensures base class is initialized before derived class.

examples.title: Using Declaration Example
examples.description: Shows how to change member accessibility in derived class
examples.explanation: Using declaration can elevate private members to public or protected in derived class. Here private inheritance is overridden to make members accessible.
